data.title: Rumble GAAP EPS of -$0.01 beats by $0.09, revenue of $23.71M beats by $0.94M
data.description:
• Rumble press release (NASDAQ:RUM): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.01 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $23.71M (+33.7% Y/Y) beats by $0.94M.

  • Outlook

    The Company continues to manage the business moving materially towards Adjusted EBITDA breakeven in 2025. With the new capital raised from Tether, we now have increased optionality to further invest in initiatives that could accelerate and expand our business.

data.title: FCC making progress on review on Verizon's purchase of Frontier - report
data.description:
Federal Communications Commission Chairman Brendan Carr is close to completing the regulator's review of Verizon's (NYSE:VZ) purchase of Frontier Communications (NASDAQ:FYBR) and wants to make progress in the next couple of weeks, according to a CTFN report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter. 

The regulator's informal 180-day shot clock on the $9.6 billion acquisition of Frontier (NASDAQ:FYBR) is set to expire today, though the deadline isn't binding, according to the report. A final decision on Friday is unlikely. 

Sources close to the process told the publication that they remain confident in FCC's eventual approval of the combination.

The latest report comes after Carr has threatened in recent months to block deals from companies that promote diversity, equity and inclusion policies. Reuters reported in late February that the FCC was reviewing the Frontier (FYBR) deal related to DEI policies.

data.title: EGH Acquisition prices $150M IPO
data.description:
• EGH Acquisition (EGHAU) announced on Thursday the  pricing of its initial public offering of 15M units at a price of $10.00 per unit. 
  • The company's units will be listed on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbol “EGHAU” and will begin trading on May 9, 2025. 
  • Each unit issued in the offering consists of one Class A ordinary share of the company and one right to receive one tenth (1/10) of a Class A ordinary share upon the consummation of the company’s initial business combination. 
  • Once the securities comprising the units begin separate trading, the Class A ordinary shares and rights are expected to be listed on NASDAQ under the symbols “EGHA” and “EGHAR,” respectively.
